6480	 * one worker, the leader often makes a very substantial contribution to
6481	 * executing the parallel portion of the plan, but as more workers are
6482	 * added, it does less and less, because it's busy reading tuples from the
6483	 * workers and doing whatever non-parallel post-processing is needed.  By
6484	 * the time we reach 4 workers, the leader no longer makes a meaningful
6485	 * contribution.  Thus, for now, estimate that the leader spends 30% of
6486	 * its time servicing each worker, and the remainder executing the
6487	 * parallel plan.
6488	 */
6489	if (parallel_leader_participation)
6490	{
6491		double		leader_contribution;
6492
6493		leader_contribution = 1.0 - (0.3 * path->parallel_workers);
6494		if (leader_contribution > 0)
6495			parallel_divisor += leader_contribution;
6496	}
6497
6498	return parallel_divisor;
6499}
6500
6501/*
6502 * compute_bitmap_pages
6503 *	  Estimate number of pages fetched from heap in a bitmap heap scan.
6504 *
6505 * 'baserel' is the relation to be scanned
6506 * 'bitmapqual' is a tree of IndexPaths, BitmapAndPaths, and BitmapOrPaths
6507 * 'loop_count' is the number of repetitions of the indexscan to factor into
6508 *		estimates of caching behavior
6509 *
6510 * If cost_p isn't NULL, the indexTotalCost estimate is returned in *cost_p.
6511 * If tuples_p isn't NULL, the tuples_fetched estimate is returned in *tuples_p.
6512 */
6513double
6514compute_bitmap_pages(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *baserel,
6515					 Path *bitmapqual, double loop_count,
6516					 Cost *cost_p, double *tuples_p)
6517{
6518	Cost		indexTotalCost;
6519	Selectivity indexSelectivity;
6520	double		T;
6521	double		pages_fetched;
6522	double		tuples_fetched;
6523	double		heap_pages;
6524	double		maxentries;
6525
6526	/*
6527	 * Fetch total cost of obtaining the bitmap, as well as its total
6528	 * selectivity.
6529	 */
6530	cost_bitmap_tree_node(bitmapqual, &indexTotalCost, &indexSelectivity);
6531
6532	/*
6533	 * Estimate number of main-table pages fetched.
6534	 */
6535	tuples_fetched = clamp_row_est(indexSelectivity * baserel->tuples);
6536
6537	T = (baserel->pages > 1) ? (double) baserel->pages : 1.0;
6538
6539	/*
6540	 * For a single scan, the number of heap pages that need to be fetched is
6541	 * the same as the Mackert and Lohman formula for the case T <= b (ie, no
6542	 * re-reads needed).
6543	 */
6544	pages_fetched = (2.0 * T * tuples_fetched) / (2.0 * T + tuples_fetched);
6545
6546	/*
6547	 * Calculate the number of pages fetched from the heap.  Then based on
6548	 * current work_mem estimate get the estimated maxentries in the bitmap.
6549	 * (Note that we always do this calculation based on the number of pages
6550	 * that would be fetched in a single iteration, even if loop_count > 1.
6551	 * That's correct, because only that number of entries will be stored in
6552	 * the bitmap at one time.)
6553	 */
6554	heap_pages = Min(pages_fetched, baserel->pages);
6555	maxentries = tbm_calculate_entries(work_mem * (Size) 1024);
6556
6557	if (loop_count > 1)
6558	{
6559		/*
6560		 * For repeated bitmap scans, scale up the number of tuples fetched in
6561		 * the Mackert and Lohman formula by the number of scans, so that we
6562		 * estimate the number of pages fetched by all the scans. Then
6563		 * pro-rate for one scan.
6564		 */
6565		pages_fetched = index_pages_fetched(tuples_fetched * loop_count,
6566											baserel->pages,
6567											get_indexpath_pages(bitmapqual),
6568											root);
6569		pages_fetched /= loop_count;
6570	}
6571
6572	if (pages_fetched >= T)
6573		pages_fetched = T;
6574	else
6575		pages_fetched = ceil(pages_fetched);
6576
6577	if (maxentries < heap_pages)
6578	{
6579		double		exact_pages;
6580		double		lossy_pages;
6581
6582		/*
6583		 * Crude approximation of the number of lossy pages.  Because of the
6584		 * way tbm_lossify() is coded, the number of lossy pages increases
6585		 * very sharply as soon as we run short of memory; this formula has
6586		 * that property and seems to perform adequately in testing, but it's
6587		 * possible we could do better somehow.
6588		 */
6589		lossy_pages = Max(0, heap_pages - maxentries / 2);
6590		exact_pages = heap_pages - lossy_pages;
6591
6592		/*
6593		 * If there are lossy pages then recompute the number of tuples
6594		 * processed by the bitmap heap node.  We assume here that the chance
6595		 * of a given tuple coming from an exact page is the same as the
6596		 * chance that a given page is exact.  This might not be true, but
6597		 * it's not clear how we can do any better.
6598		 */
6599		if (lossy_pages > 0)
6600			tuples_fetched =
6601				clamp_row_est(indexSelectivity *
6602							  (exact_pages / heap_pages) * baserel->tuples +
6603							  (lossy_pages / heap_pages) * baserel->tuples);
6604	}
6605
6606	if (cost_p)
6607		*cost_p = indexTotalCost;
6608	if (tuples_p)
6609		*tuples_p = tuples_fetched;
6610
6611	return pages_fetched;
6612}
6613
6614/*
6615 * compute_gather_rows
6616 *	  Estimate number of rows for gather (merge) nodes.
6617 *
6618 * In a parallel plan, each worker's row estimate is determined by dividing the
6619 * total number of rows by parallel_divisor, which accounts for the leader's
6620 * contribution in addition to the number of workers.  Accordingly, when
6621 * estimating the number of rows for gather (merge) nodes, we multiply the rows
6622 * per worker by the same parallel_divisor to undo the division.
6623 */
6624double
6625compute_gather_rows(Path *path)
6626{
6627	Assert(path->parallel_workers > 0);
6628
6629	return clamp_row_est(path->rows * get_parallel_divisor(path));
6630}
